title: Artemis II heat shield aced its blistering reentry, ghostly underwater photo reveals
summary: The Orion heat shield used for the Artemis II mission held up perfectly, early photos and a NASA assessment reveal.

title: توقيف 14 شخص بتطوان يشتبه في ارتباطهم بشبكة إجرامية
summary: تمكنت عناصر فرقة مكافحة العصابات بولاية أمن تطوان بناء على معلومات وفرتها مصالح المديرية العامة لمراقبة التراب الوطني، صباح اليوم الخميس 23 أبريل الجاري، من توقيف 14 شخصا يشتبه في ارتباطهم بشبكة إجرامية تنشط في ترويج المخدرات والمؤثرات العقلية. وقد تم تنفيذ هذه العملية الأمنية بالمنطقةأكمل القراءة …
body: تمكنت عناصر فرقة مكافحة العصابات بولاية أمن تطوان بناء على معلومات وفرتها مصالح المديرية العامة لمراقبة التراب الوطني، صباح اليوم الخميس 23 أبريل الجاري، من توقيف 14 شخصا يشتبه في ارتباطهم بشبكة إجرامية تنشط في ترويج المخدرات والمؤثرات العقلية. وقد تم تنفيذ هذه العملية الأمنية بالمنطقة أكمل القراءة » المقالة توقيف 14 شخص بتطوان يشتبه في ارتباطهم بشبكة إجرامية fمنشورة على جريدة الصباح .

title: What 6,000 researchers think about the future of science
summary:
Nature, Published online: 23 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01061-2

Funding worries and publishing trends loom large in the minds of scientists surveyed by Nature Index.
body: Nature, Published online: 23 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01061-2 Funding worries and publishing trends loom large in the minds of scientists surveyed by Nature Index.

title: Delays have kept new NSF grants to a trickle — that could be about to change
summary:
Nature, Published online: 23 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01287-0

The agency is finally ready to distribute a spate of new grants, but it is also preparing substantial cuts to areas such as biological and social sciences.
body: Nature, Published online: 23 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01287-0 The agency is finally ready to distribute a spate of new grants, but it is also preparing substantial cuts to areas such as biological and social sciences.

title: NASA's Curiosity rover finds a surprising number of giant 'dragon scales' littered across Mars
summary: The Curiosity rover snapped a series of peculiar polygons that look suspiciously like giant fossilized reptile scales. Although scientists have seen similar shapes on Mars before, they have never seen such a "dramatically abundant" concentration.

title: Earth quiz: What do you know about our planet's most amazing features?
summary: Do you know Earth's highest peak, deepest point, and more? Test your knowledge about our planet in this quiz.

title: Symmetry classification of magnetic orders using oriented spin space groups
summary:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10401-1

An oriented spin space group description is proposed that unifies the spin space group and magnetic space group frameworks, enabling a comprehensive symmetr…
body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10401-1 An oriented spin space group description is proposed that unifies the spin space group and magnetic space group frameworks, enabling a comprehensive symmetry classification of the magnetic materials on a large scale and identifying spin–orbit magnetism as a distinct phase.

title: Meeting the moment: how scientific philanthropies are expanding their reach
summary:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01043-4

As public funding continues to shift under researchers’ feet, could charitable organizations hold the key to preserving scientific research?
body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01043-4 As public funding continues to shift under researchers’ feet, could charitable organizations hold the key to preserving scientific research?

title: NASA shuts off another Voyager 1 instrument as humanity's most distant spacecraft prepares for risky 'Big Bang' maneuver to save power
summary: After nearly 50 years in space, the two Voyager spacecraft are very low on nuclear power. Voyager 1 just shut off another instrument to save the mission.

title: Neanderthal toddlers grew faster than modern humans, probably because of the harsh environment they evolved in
summary: A new study of a Neanderthal toddler reveals that our closest evolutionary relatives' growth patterns differed from those of modern humans.

title: Glowing ring of plankton surrounding New Zealand islands linked to deadly underwater plateau — Earth from space
summary: A recent satellite photo captured a gleaming halo of phytoplankton encircling the remote Chatham Islands. The stunning sight is the result of a hidden underwater structure, which has also helped kill hundreds of cetaceans.

title: Bruce the parrot is missing his upper beak —‬ but that hasn't stopped him from becoming an undefeated jousting champion
summary: A kea parrot in New Zealand who lost the top part of his beak when young has developed a new way to fight other males that has made him unbeatable.

title: Altar to Sol: A rare 1,900-year-old monument dedicated to the Roman god of light and used in a secret underground ritual
summary: This unique carved altar represents the triumph of light over darkness in ancient Roman religion.

title: ‘Bat feast’ animal videos at African cave offer clues to how deadly viruses spread
summary:
Nature, Published online: 20 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01259-4

Researchers filmed 10 species eating or scavenging bats at known Marburg-virus hotspot — and caught hundreds of humans visiting.
body: Nature, Published online: 20 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01259-4 Researchers filmed 10 species eating or scavenging bats at known Marburg-virus hotspot — and caught hundreds of humans visiting.

title: Naked mole rats wage bloody wars of succession to choose a new queen — but one colony did something scientists have never seen before
summary: When their queen dies, naked mole rat females usually wage bloody battles of succession. But peace may be possible, a new study suggests

title: Can chickens really run around with their heads cut off?
summary: There's lore about chickens surviving from seconds to months after their heads are chopped off, but what does the science say?

title: Archaeologists discover perfectly circular ancient Egyptian temple that may have been used for sacred water rituals
summary: The temple included a network of water infrastructure, revealing the importance of the ancient city and the Nile.

title: Some polar bears are adapting to their melting habitat. Will it be enough to save the iconic species?
summary: Bears in Svalbard, Norway, are fatter than expected, and others in Greenland are showing signs of genetically adapting to climate change — but the signs elsewhere are not good.

title: 2 supermassive black holes may collide 100 years from now ‪—‬ and Earth would feel it
summary: In a galaxy 500 million light-years away, two supermassive black holes could merge, spreading gravitational waves across the universe.

title: Anglo-Saxon burial holds an older sister cradling her little brother after they both died 1,400 years ago, possibly of an infectious disease
summary: An ancient-DNA analysis of a rare Anglo-Saxon double burial revealed the people in the grave were brother and sister.

title: Colorado River may have pooled and spilled over to form the Grand Canyon, solving a long-standing mystery ‪—‬ but not everyone agrees
summary: The Colorado River muscled its way through today's Grand Canyon after pooling as a giant lake, according to new research.

title: The first black hole ever discovered is spewing 'dancing jets' at half the speed of light
summary: Astronomers have accurately measured the "dancing" energy jets of the first confirmed black hole, Cygnus X-1, more than 60 years after it was first spotted.

title: Stephen Hawking's black hole information paradox could be solved — if the universe has 7 dimensions
summary: Stephen Hawking's theory of black hole evaporation clashes with the laws of quantum mechanics. A new paper finds a way around this paradox, provided that the universe has seven dimensions.

title: Artemis II quiz: Is your knowledge of NASA's historic moon mission out of this world?
summary: The slingshot around the moon has catapulted the Artemis II mission to legendary status. How much do you know about this iconic mission?

title: فيديو.. هبوط رواد "أرتيميس 2" في مياه المحيط الهادئ
summary: هبطت مركبة مهمة "أرتيميس 2" التي حملت 4 رواد فضاء حول القمر، الجمعة، في مياه المحيط الهادئ مقابل سواحل كاليفورنيا، كما هو مخطط لها.

title: على شكل "كرة نار".. رواد الفضاء يستعدون للعودة إلى الأرض
summary: يستعد رواد الفضاء الذين أرسلتهم وكالة الفضاء الأميركية "ناسا" إلى القمر في إطار مهمة "أرتيمس 2" للعودة إلى الأرض، وذلك بعد انطلاقهم في مطلع أبريل الجاري.

title: 10 ملايين دولار.. مكافأة أميركية لاعتقال شقيقين يقودان عصابة
summary: قالت وزارة الخارجية الأميركية يوم الخميس إنها ستدفع ما يصل إلى 10 ملايين دولار مقابل معلومات تؤدي إلى اعتقال أو إدانة شقيقين تم تحديدهما كزعيمين لعصابة سينالوا المكسيكية في ولاية باها كاليفورنيا، التي تضم مدينة تيخوانا.
